content.content:
1→// Package model defines shared domain types used across the control plane.
     2→//
     3→// These types are the canonical Go representations of the structures defined
     4→// in the OpenAPI specification. All SPI packages import from here rather than
     5→// defining their own copies.
     6→package model
     7→
     8→import "time"
     9→
    10→// ActorRef identifies who or what performed an action.
    11→type ActorRef struct {
    12→	ID          string
    13→	Type        ActorType
    14→	DisplayName string
    15→}
    16→
    17→// ActorType classifies the actor.
    18→type ActorType string
    19→
    20→const (
    21→	ActorTypeAgent            ActorType = "agent"
    22→	ActorTypeHuman            ActorType = "human"
    23→	ActorTypeService          ActorType = "service"
    24→	ActorTypeConnectorRuntime ActorType = "connector_runtime"
    25→)
    26→
    27→// ActionTarget describes the resource an action operates on.
    28→type ActionTarget struct {
    29→	Kind        string
    30→	ID          string
    31→	DisplayName string
    32→}
    33→
    34→// ActionIntent describes what an agent intends to do.
    35→type ActionIntent struct {
    36→	// Type is a dot-namespaced action identifier, e.g. "payment.charge",
    37→	// "git.pull_request.create", "calendar.event.create".
    38→	Type          string
    39→	Summary       string
    40→	Justification string
    41→	Target        ActionTarget
    42→	Domain        DomainAction
    43→	Metadata      map[string]any
    44→}
    45→
    46→// DomainAction carries action-type-specific fields.
    47→// Exactly one field should be populated, corresponding to the ActionIntent.Type prefix.
    48→type DomainAction struct {
    49→	Git         *GitAction
    50→	Deploy      *DeployAction
    51→	Cloud       *CloudAction
    52→	Email       *EmailAction
    53→	Calendar    *CalendarAction
    54→	Payment     *PaymentAction
    55→	Procurement *ProcurementAction
    56→}
    57→
    58→// Money represents an amount in minor units (e.g. cents) with a currency code.
    59→type Money struct {
    60→	Amount   int64
    61→	Currency string // ISO 4217, e.g. "USD"
    62→}
    63→
    64→// GitAction carries git/source-control-specific intent fields.
    65→type GitAction struct {
    66→	Provider       string // "github", "gitlab", "bitbucket", "custom"
    67→	Repository     string
    68→	Branch         string
    69→	BaseBranch     string
    70→	CommitSHAs     []string
    71→	FilesChanged   []string
    72→	PRTitle        string
    73→	PRBody         string
    74→	Labels         []string
    75→	Reviewers      []string
    76→	ChecksRequired []string
    77→}
    78→
    79→// DeployAction carries deployment-specific intent fields.
    80→type DeployAction struct {
    81→	Provider            string // "kubernetes", "vercel", "netlify", etc.
    82→	Service             string
    83→	Environment         string
    84→	ArtifactRef         string
    85→	ImageRef            string
    86→	Cluster             string
    87→	Namespace           string
    88→	Strategy            string // "rolling", "blue_green", "canary", "replace", "custom"
    89→	RollbackSupported   bool
    90→	ChangeTicketID      string
    91→	MaintenanceWindowID string
    92→}
    93→
    94→// CloudAction carries cloud-resource-specific intent fields.
    95→type CloudAction struct {
    96→	Provider      string // "aws", "gcp", "azure", "cloudflare", "custom"
    97→	AccountID     string
    98→	Region        string
    99→	ResourceType  string
   100→	ResourceID    string
   101→	Operation     string
   102→	EstimatedCost *Money
   103→	Tags          map[string]string
   104→}
   105→
   106→// EmailAction carries email-specific intent fields.
   107→type EmailAction struct {
   108→	From        *Recipient
   109→	To          []Recipient
   110→	CC          []Recipient
   111→	BCC         []Recipient
   112→	Subject     string
   113→	BodyText    string
   114→	BodyHTML    string
   115→	Attachments []AttachmentRef
   116→	ThreadRef   string
   117→	SendMode    string // "send_now", "draft_only"
   118→}
   119→
   120→// Recipient identifies an email participant.
   121→type Recipient struct {
   122→	Name  string
   123→	Email string
   124→}
   125→
   126→// AttachmentRef references a file attachment.
   127→type AttachmentRef struct {
   128→	Name       string
   129→	MIMEType   string
   130→	URL        string
   131→	StorageRef string
   132→}
   133→
   134→// CalendarAction carries calendar-specific intent fields.
   135→type CalendarAction struct {
   136→	Provider       string // "google_calendar", "outlook", "custom"
   137→	Title          string
   138→	Description    string
   139→	Attendees      []CalendarAttendee
   140→	StartTime      *time.Time
   141→	EndTime        *time.Time
   142→	Timezone       string
   143→	Location       string
   144→	ConferenceType string // "none", "google_meet", "zoom", "teams", "custom"
   145→	CalendarID     string
   146→	Visibility     string // "default", "public", "private"
   147→}
   148→
   149→// CalendarAttendee is a participant in a calendar event.
   150→type CalendarAttendee struct {
   151→	Name     string
   152→	Email    string
   153→	Optional bool
   154→}
   155→
   156→// PaymentAction carries payment-specific intent fields.
   157→type PaymentAction struct {
   158→	VendorName                   string
   159→	VendorID                     string
   160→	Amount                       Money
   161→	MerchantCategory             string
   162→	BudgetCode                   string
   163→	FundingSourceID              string
   164→	PaymentInstrumentPreference  string // "virtual_card", "ach", "wallet", "network_proxy", "unspecified"
   165→	Beneficiary                  *PaymentBeneficiary
   166→	LineItems                    []LineItem
   167→	Renewal                      bool
   168→	ContractTerm                 string
   169→	RecurringInterval            string // "none", "monthly", "quarterly", "annual"
   170→	MerchantReference            string
   171→}
   172→
   173→// PaymentBeneficiary identifies the person or team benefiting from a payment.
   174→type PaymentBeneficiary struct {
   175→	Name       string
   176→	Email      string
   177→	Department string
   178→}
   179→
   180→// LineItem is a single item in a payment or procurement request.
   181→type LineItem struct {
   182→	Description string
   183→	Quantity    int
   184→	UnitAmount  Money
   185→	SKU         string
   186→}
   187→
   188→// ProcurementAction carries procurement-specific intent fields.
   189→type ProcurementAction struct {
   190→	RequestType            string // "software", "travel", "equipment", "contractor", "services", "other"
   191→	VendorName             string
   192→	AmountEstimate         *Money
   193→	Justification          string
   194→	Requestor              string
   195→	CostCenter             string
   196→	LegalReviewRequired    bool
   197→	SecurityReviewRequired bool
   198→	LineItems              []LineItem
   199→}
   200→
   201→// IntentContext carries runtime context used by policy rules.
   202→type IntentContext struct {
   203→	Environment      string
   204→	SourcePlatform   string
   205→	SourceSessionID  string
   206→	SourceTraceID    string
   207→	IPAddress        string
   208→	UserPresent      bool
   209→	RiskHints        []string
   210→	TemporaryGrantID string
   211→}
   212→
   213→// Disposition is the outcome of policy evaluation.
   214→type Disposition string
   215→
   216→const (
   217→	DispositionAllow               Disposition = "allow"
   218→	DispositionAllowModified       Disposition = "allow_modified"
   219→	DispositionRequireApproval     Disposition = "require_approval"
   220→	DispositionDeny                Disposition = "deny"
   221→	DispositionRequireMoreEvidence Disposition = "require_more_evidence"
   222→)
   223→
   224→// RiskLevel is the assessed risk of an intent.
   225→type RiskLevel string
   226→
   227→const (
   228→	RiskLevelLow      RiskLevel = "low"
   229→	RiskLevelMedium   RiskLevel = "medium"
   230→	RiskLevelHigh     RiskLevel = "high"
   231→	RiskLevelCritical RiskLevel = "critical"
   232→)
   233→
   234→// Decision is the output of policy evaluation.
   235→type Decision struct {
   236→	Disposition     Disposition
   237→	RiskLevel       RiskLevel
   238→	MatchedPolicies []PolicyMatch
   239→	DenialReason    string
   240→}
   241→
   242→// PolicyMatch records which policy rule produced a decision.
   243→type PolicyMatch struct {
   244→	PolicyID      string
   245→	PolicyVersion int
   246→	RuleID        string
   247→	Explanation   string
   248→}
   249→
   250→// EventType identifies the kind of audit event.
   251→type EventType string
   252→
   253→const (
   254→	EventTypeIntentSubmitted    EventType = "intent.submitted"
   255→	EventTypePolicyEvaluated    EventType = "policy.evaluated"
   256→	EventTypeApprovalRequested  EventType = "approval.requested"
   257→	EventTypeApprovalApproved   EventType = "approval.approved"
   258→	EventTypeApprovalDenied     EventType = "approval.denied"
   259→	EventTypeApprovalModified   EventType = "approval.modified"
   260→	EventTypeGrantIssued        EventType = "grant.issued"
   261→	EventTypeGrantRevoked       EventType = "grant.revoked"
   262→	EventTypeExecutionStarted   EventType = "execution.started"
   263→	EventTypeExecutionSucceeded EventType = "execution.succeeded"
   264→	EventTypeExecutionFailed    EventType = "execution.failed"
   265→
   266→	// Gateway tool call events.
   267→	EventTypeToolCallIntercepted    EventType = "tool.call.intercepted"
   268→	EventTypeToolCallForwarded      EventType = "tool.call.forwarded"
   269→	EventTypeToolCallDenied         EventType = "tool.call.denied"
   270→	EventTypeToolCallPendingApproval EventType = "tool.call.pending_approval"
   271→)

1→// Package store defines persistence interfaces for the control plane.
     2→//
     3→// These interfaces decouple the API handlers from the storage backend.
     4→// The in-memory implementations (in the mem sub-package) are suitable for
     5→// development and testing. Production deployments swap in Postgres-backed
     6→// implementations without changing business logic.
     7→package store
     8→
     9→import (
    10→	"context"
    11→
    12→	api "github.com/ALRubinger/aileron/core/api/gen"
    13→)
    14→
    15→// IntentStore persists and retrieves action intents.
    16→type IntentStore interface {
    17→	Create(ctx context.Context, intent api.IntentEnvelope) error
    18→	Get(ctx context.Context, intentID string) (api.IntentEnvelope, error)
    19→	List(ctx context.Context, filter IntentFilter) ([]api.IntentEnvelope, error)
    20→	Update(ctx context.Context, intent api.IntentEnvelope) error
    21→}
    22→
    23→// IntentFilter scopes an intent list query.
    24→type IntentFilter struct {
    25→	WorkspaceID string
    26→	Status      *api.IntentStatus
    27→	AgentID     string
    28→	PageSize    int
    29→	PageToken   string
    30→}
    31→
    32→// ApprovalStore persists and retrieves approval requests.
    33→type ApprovalStore interface {
    34→	Create(ctx context.Context, approval api.Approval) error
    35→	Get(ctx context.Context, approvalID string) (api.Approval, error)
    36→	List(ctx context.Context, filter ApprovalFilter) ([]api.Approval, error)
    37→	Update(ctx context.Context, approval api.Approval) error
    38→}
    39→
    40→// ApprovalFilter scopes an approval list query.
    41→type ApprovalFilter struct {
    42→	WorkspaceID string
    43→	Status      *api.ApprovalStatus
    44→	IntentID    string
    45→	PageSize    int
    46→	PageToken   string
    47→}
    48→
    49→// PolicyStore persists and retrieves policy definitions.
    50→type PolicyStore interface {
    51→	Create(ctx context.Context, policy api.Policy) error
    52→	Get(ctx context.Context, policyID string) (api.Policy, error)
    53→	List(ctx context.Context, filter PolicyFilter) ([]api.Policy, error)
    54→	Update(ctx context.Context, policy api.Policy) error
    55→}
    56→
    57→// PolicyFilter scopes a policy list query.
    58→type PolicyFilter struct {
    59→	WorkspaceID string
    60→	Status      *api.PolicyStatus
    61→	PageSize    int
    62→	PageToken   string
    63→}
    64→
    65→// GrantStore persists and retrieves execution grants.
    66→type GrantStore interface {
    67→	Create(ctx context.Context, grant api.ExecutionGrant) error
    68→	Get(ctx context.Context, grantID string) (api.ExecutionGrant, error)
    69→	Update(ctx context.Context, grant api.ExecutionGrant) error
    70→}
    71→
    72→// ExecutionStore persists and retrieves execution records.
    73→type ExecutionStore interface {
    74→	Create(ctx context.Context, execution api.Execution) error
    75→	Get(ctx context.Context, executionID string) (api.Execution, error)
    76→	Update(ctx context.Context, execution api.Execution) error
    77→}
    78→
    79→// ConnectorStore persists and retrieves connector registrations.
    80→type ConnectorStore interface {
    81→	Create(ctx context.Context, connector api.Connector) error
    82→	Get(ctx context.Context, connectorID string) (api.Connector, error)
    83→	List(ctx context.Context, filter ConnectorFilter) ([]api.Connector, error)
    84→	Update(ctx context.Context, connector api.Connector) error
    85→}
    86→
    87→// ConnectorFilter scopes a connector list query.
    88→type ConnectorFilter struct {
    89→	WorkspaceID string
    90→	Type        *api.ConnectorType
    91→	PageSize    int
    92→	PageToken   string
    93→}
    94→
    95→// CredentialStore persists and retrieves credential references (not secrets).
    96→type CredentialStore interface {
    97→	Create(ctx context.Context, cred api.CredentialReference) error
    98→	Get(ctx context.Context, credentialID string) (api.CredentialReference, error)
    99→	List(ctx context.Context, filter CredentialFilter) ([]api.CredentialReference, error)
   100→}
   101→
   102→// CredentialFilter scopes a credential list query.
   103→type CredentialFilter struct {
   104→	WorkspaceID string
   105→	PageSize    int
   106→	PageToken   string
   107→}
   108→
   109→// FundingSourceStore persists and retrieves funding sources.
   110→type FundingSourceStore interface {
   111→	Create(ctx context.Context, fs api.FundingSource) error
   112→	Get(ctx context.Context, fundingSourceID string) (api.FundingSource, error)
   113→	List(ctx context.Context, filter FundingSourceFilter) ([]api.FundingSource, error)
   114→}
   115→
   116→// FundingSourceFilter scopes a funding source list query.
   117→type FundingSourceFilter struct {
   118→	WorkspaceID string
   119→	PageSize    int
   120→	PageToken   string
   121→}
   122→
   123→// MCPServerStore persists and retrieves MCP server configurations.
   124→type MCPServerStore interface {
   125→	Create(ctx context.Context, server api.MCPServerConfig) error
   126→	Get(ctx context.Context, serverID string) (api.MCPServerConfig, error)
   127→	List(ctx context.Context, filter MCPServerFilter) ([]api.MCPServerConfig, error)
   128→	Update(ctx context.Context, server api.MCPServerConfig) error
   129→	Delete(ctx context.Context, serverID string) error
   130→}
   131→
   132→// MCPServerFilter scopes an MCP server list query.
   133→type MCPServerFilter struct {
   134→	Status   *api.MCPServerConfigStatus
   135→	PageSize int
   136→}
   137→
   138→// ErrNotFound is returned when a requested entity does not exist.
   139→type ErrNotFound struct {
   140→	Entity string
   141→	ID     string
   142→}
   143→
   144→func (e *ErrNotFound) Error() string {
   145→	return e.Entity + " not found: " + e.ID
   146→}

1→// Package config loads and validates YAML configuration for the Aileron
     2→// MCP gateway.
     3→//
     4→// A configuration file defines the set of downstream MCP servers that
     5→// Aileron proxies, along with optional policy mappings and environment
     6→// variable overrides. Environment values prefixed with "vault://" are
     7→// resolved from the Aileron vault at launch time via [ResolveVaultEnv].
     8→package config
     9→
    10→import (
    11→	"fmt"
    12→	"os"
    13→	"regexp"
    14→
    15→	"gopkg.in/yaml.v3"
    16→)
    17→
    18→// namePattern matches valid downstream server names: starts with a letter,
    19→// followed by zero or more alphanumeric characters or underscores.
    20→var namePattern = regexp.MustCompile(`^[a-zA-Z][a-zA-Z0-9_]*$`)
    21→
    22→// vaultPrefix is the sentinel prefix for environment variable values that
    23→// should be resolved from the vault.
    24→const vaultPrefix = "vault://"
    25→
    26→// Execution mode constants for downstream servers.
    27→const (
    28→	// ModeLocal runs the MCP server as a local subprocess (stdio transport).
    29→	// This is the default when Mode is omitted.
    30→	ModeLocal = "local"
    31→
    32→	// ModeRemote connects to a centrally-managed MCP server over HTTP
    33→	// (Streamable HTTP transport). The control plane manages the server
    34→	// instance; no local command is needed.
    35→	ModeRemote = "remote"
    36→)
    37→
    38→// Config is the top-level Aileron gateway configuration.
    39→type Config struct {
    40→	// Version is the config file format version. Currently "1".
    41→	Version string `yaml:"version"`
    42→
    43→	// WorkspaceID is the default workspace for policy evaluation.
    44→	// Defaults to "default" if empty.
    45→	WorkspaceID string `yaml:"workspace_id"`
    46→
    47→	// DownstreamServers lists the MCP servers that Aileron proxies.
    48→	DownstreamServers []DownstreamServer `yaml:"downstream_servers"`
    49→}
    50→
    51→// DownstreamServer defines a single downstream MCP server.
    52→type DownstreamServer struct {
    53→	// Name is a unique identifier for this server, used as tool name prefix.
    54→	// Must be a valid identifier (alphanumeric + underscore, no spaces).
    55→	Name string `yaml:"name"`
    56→
    57→	// Mode is the execution mode: "local" (default) or "remote".
    58→	// Local servers are spawned as subprocesses; remote servers are accessed
    59→	// over HTTP via the control plane. Omitting Mode defaults to "local".
    60→	Mode string `yaml:"mode,omitempty"`
    61→
    62→	// Command is the command to execute, including any arguments.
    63→	// Required for local mode; must be empty for remote mode.
    64→	// Example: ["npx", "-y", "@modelcontextprotocol/server-github"]
    65→	Command []string `yaml:"command"`
    66→
    67→	// Env is environment variables to set for the subprocess.
    68→	// Values prefixed with "vault://" are resolved from the Aileron vault at launch time.
    69→	Env map[string]string `yaml:"env"`
    70→
    71→	// PolicyMapping configures how this server's tools map to policy evaluation.
    72→	PolicyMapping *PolicyMapping `yaml:"policy_mapping,omitempty"`
    73→}
    74→
    75→// PolicyMapping configures how tools from a downstream server map to the
    76→// policy engine's action type namespace.
    77→type PolicyMapping struct {
    78→	// ToolPrefix maps tool calls to the action type namespace.
    79→	// For example, with tool_prefix "git", a tool "create_pull_request"
    80→	// becomes action type "git.create_pull_request" for policy evaluation.
    81→	ToolPrefix string `yaml:"tool_prefix"`
    82→}
    83→
    84→// Load reads a YAML configuration file from path, unmarshals it, and
    85→// validates the result. It returns the parsed Config or the first error
    86→// encountered.
    87→func Load(path string) (*Config, error) {
    88→	data, err := os.ReadFile(path)
    89→	if err != nil {
    90→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("reading config file: %w", err)
    91→	}
    92→
    93→	var cfg Config
    94→	if err := yaml.Unmarshal(data, &cfg); err != nil {
    95→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("parsing config file: %w", err)
    96→	}
    97→
    98→	if err := cfg.Validate(); err != nil {
    99→		return nil, err
   100→	}
   101→
   102→	return &cfg, nil
   103→}
   104→
   105→// LoadFromEnvOrDefault loads configuration using the following precedence:
   106→//
   107→//  1. The path in the AILERON_CONFIG environment variable.
   108→//  2. "aileron.yaml" in the current working directory.
   109→//  3. An empty Config (no downstream servers) — this is not an error; it
   110→//     allows the gateway to start with no downstream servers configured.
   111→func LoadFromEnvOrDefault() (*Config, error) {
   112→	if path := os.Getenv("AILERON_CONFIG"); path != "" {
   113→		return Load(path)
   114→	}
   115→
   116→	const defaultFile = "aileron.yaml"
   117→	if _, err := os.Stat(defaultFile); err == nil {
   118→		return Load(defaultFile)
   119→	}
   120→
   121→	return &Config{}, nil
   122→}
   123→
   124→// Validate checks the configuration for structural correctness. It returns
   125→// a descriptive error for the first validation failure found.
   126→func (c *Config) Validate() error {
   127→	if c.Version != "" && c.Version != "1" {
   128→		return fmt.Errorf("unsupported config version %q: must be \"1\"", c.Version)
   129→	}
   130→
   131→	seen := make(map[string]bool, len(c.DownstreamServers))
   132→	for i, ds := range c.DownstreamServers {
   133→		if ds.Name == "" {
   134→			return fmt.Errorf("downstream_servers[%d]: name must not be empty", i)
   135→		}
   136→		if !namePattern.MatchString(ds.Name) {
   137→			return fmt.Errorf("downstream_servers[%d]: name %q must match %s", i, ds.Name, namePattern.String())
   138→		}
   139→		if seen[ds.Name] {
   140→			return fmt.Errorf("downstream_servers[%d]: duplicate name %q", i, ds.Name)
   141→		}
   142→		seen[ds.Name] = true
   143→
   144→		// Normalize and validate mode.
   145→		mode := ds.Mode
   146→		if mode == "" {
   147→			mode = ModeLocal
   148→		}
   149→		if mode != ModeLocal && mode != ModeRemote {
   150→			return fmt.Errorf("downstream_servers[%d] (%s): invalid mode %q: must be %q or %q", i, ds.Name, ds.Mode, ModeLocal, ModeRemote)
   151→		}
   152→
   153→		// Local servers require a command; remote servers must not have one.
   154→		if mode == ModeLocal && len(ds.Command) == 0 {
   155→			return fmt.Errorf("downstream_servers[%d] (%s): command must not be empty for local mode", i, ds.Name)
   156→		}
   157→		if mode == ModeRemote && len(ds.Command) > 0 {
   158→			return fmt.Errorf("downstream_servers[%d] (%s): command must be empty for remote mode", i, ds.Name)
   159→		}
   160→	}
   161→
   162→	return nil
   163→}
   164→
   165→// ResolveVaultEnv returns a copy of env with all "vault://"-prefixed values
   166→// resolved through the provided vaultGet function. Non-vault values are
   167→// copied unchanged. The original map is never modified.
   168→func ResolveVaultEnv(env map[string]string, vaultGet func(path string) ([]byte, error)) (map[string]string, error) {
   169→	resolved := make(map[string]string, len(env))
   170→
   171→	for k, v := range env {
   172→		if len(v) > len(vaultPrefix) && v[:len(vaultPrefix)] == vaultPrefix {
   173→			vaultPath := v[len(vaultPrefix):]
   174→			secret, err := vaultGet(vaultPath)
   175→			if err != nil {
   176→				return nil, fmt.Errorf("resolving vault env %q (path %q): %w", k, vaultPath, err)
   177→			}
   178→			resolved[k] = string(secret)
   179→		} else {
   180→			resolved[k] = v
   181→		}
   182→	}
   183→
   184→	return resolved, nil
   185→}
   186→

1→// Package app wires together the Aileron control plane components and exposes
     2→// them as an http.Handler. It is imported by the standalone server binary and
     3→// the MCP server's embedded mode.
     4→package app
     5→
     6→import (
     7→	"context"
     8→	"log/slog"
     9→	"net/http"
    10→	"os"
    11→	"time"
    12→
    13→	api "github.com/ALRubinger/aileron/core/api/gen"
    14→	"github.com/ALRubinger/aileron/core/approval"
    15→	"github.com/ALRubinger/aileron/core/connector"
    16→	googlecalendar "github.com/ALRubinger/aileron/core/connector/calendar/google"
    17→	"github.com/ALRubinger/aileron/core/connector/git/github"
    18→	"github.com/ALRubinger/aileron/core/connector/payments/stripe"
    19→	"github.com/ALRubinger/aileron/core/notify"
    20→	"github.com/ALRubinger/aileron/core/policy"
    21→	mcpreg "github.com/ALRubinger/aileron/core/registry"
    22→	"github.com/ALRubinger/aileron/core/store/mem"
    23→	"github.com/ALRubinger/aileron/core/vault"
    24→	"github.com/google/uuid"
    25→)
    26→
    27→// NewHandler creates a fully-wired Aileron control plane HTTP handler with
    28→// in-memory stores, seeded policies, and registered connectors.
    29→func NewHandler(log *slog.Logger) (http.Handler, error) {
    30→	ctx := context.Background()
    31→
    32→	// --- In-memory stores ---
    33→	intentStore := mem.NewIntentStore()
    34→	approvalStore := mem.NewApprovalStore()
    35→	policyStore := mem.NewPolicyStore()
    36→	grantStore := mem.NewGrantStore()
    37→	executionStore := mem.NewExecutionStore()
    38→	connectorStore := mem.NewConnectorStore()
    39→	mcpServerStore := mem.NewMCPServerStore()
    40→	credentialStore := mem.NewCredentialStore()
    41→	fundingSourceStore := mem.NewFundingSourceStore()
    42→	traceStore := mem.NewTraceStore()
    43→
    44→	// --- Connector registry ---
    45→	registry := connector.NewRegistry()
    46→	registry.Register(ctx, stripe.New())
    47→	registry.Register(ctx, googlecalendar.New())
    48→	registry.Register(ctx, github.New())
    49→
    50→	// --- Vault ---
    51→	v := vault.NewMemVault()
    52→	if token := os.Getenv("GITHUB_TOKEN"); token != "" {
    53→		v.Put(ctx, "connectors/github/default", []byte(token), vault.Metadata{
    54→			Type: "api_key",
    55→			Labels: map[string]string{
    56→				"connector": "github",
    57→			},
    58→		})
    59→		log.Info("seeded GitHub token into vault")
    60→	}
    61→
    62→	// --- Policy engine ---
    63→	policyEngine := policy.NewRuleEngine(policyStore)
    64→
    65→	if err := policy.SeedPolicies(ctx, policyStore); err != nil {
    66→		return nil, err
    67→	}
    68→	log.Info("seeded default policies")
    69→
    70→	// --- Approval orchestrator ---
    71→	idGen := func() string { return uuid.New().String() }
    72→	orchestrator := approval.NewInMemoryOrchestrator(approvalStore, idGen)
    73→
    74→	// --- MCP Registry client ---
    75→	registryClient := mcpreg.NewClient(nil, log)
    76→	if interval := os.Getenv("REGISTRY_REFRESH_INTERVAL"); interval != "" {
    77→		if d, err := time.ParseDuration(interval); err == nil {
    78→			registryClient = registryClient.WithRefreshInterval(d)
    79→			log.Info("registry refresh interval overridden", "interval", d)
    80→		} else {
    81→			log.Warn("invalid REGISTRY_REFRESH_INTERVAL, using default", "value", interval, "error", err)
    82→		}
    83→	}
    84→	registryClient.Start(ctx)
    85→
    86→	// --- Notifier ---
    87→	notifier := notify.NewLogNotifier(log)
    88→
    89→	// --- HTTP handler ---
    90→	mux := http.NewServeMux()
    91→
    92→	server := &apiServer{
    93→		log:            log,
    94→		registry:       registry,
    95→		policyEngine:   policyEngine,
    96→		orchestrator:   orchestrator,
    97→		vault:          v,
    98→		notifier:       notifier,
    99→		intents:        intentStore,
   100→		approvals:      approvalStore,
   101→		policies:       policyStore,
   102→		grants:         grantStore,
   103→		executions:     executionStore,
   104→		connectors:     connectorStore,
   105→		mcpServers:     mcpServerStore,
   106→		registryClient: registryClient,
   107→		credentials:    credentialStore,
   108→		fundingSources: fundingSourceStore,
   109→		traces:         traceStore,
   110→		newID:          idGen,
   111→	}
   112→	api.HandlerFromMux(server, mux)
   113→
   114→	registerDocsRoutes(mux)
   115→
   116→	// Middleware chain: CORS -> request ID -> logging -> routes.
   117→	var handler http.Handler = mux
   118→	handler = loggingMiddleware(log, handler)
   119→	handler = requestIDMiddleware(handler)
   120→	handler = corsMiddleware(handler)
   121→
   122→	return handler, nil
   123→}
   124→

1→package app
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"context"
     5→	"crypto/rand"
     6→	"encoding/hex"
     7→	"log/slog"
     8→	"net/http"
     9→	"time"
    10→)
    11→
    12→type contextKey int
    13→
    14→const requestIDKey contextKey = iota
    15→
    16→// RequestID returns the request ID from the context, or empty string if none.
    17→func RequestID(ctx context.Context) string {
    18→	id, _ := ctx.Value(requestIDKey).(string)
    19→	return id
    20→}
    21→
    22→// requestIDMiddleware injects a unique request ID into each request context
    23→// and sets it on the response. If the client sends X-Request-ID, it is reused.
    24→func requestIDMiddleware(next http.Handler) http.Handler {
    25→	return http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
    26→		id := r.Header.Get("X-Request-ID")
    27→		if id == "" {
    28→			id = generateID()
    29→		}
    30→		w.Header().Set("X-Request-ID", id)
    31→		ctx := context.WithValue(r.Context(), requestIDKey, id)
    32→		next.ServeHTTP(w, r.WithContext(ctx))
    33→	})
    34→}
    35→
    36→// loggingMiddleware logs each request with method, path, status, and duration.
    37→func loggingMiddleware(log *slog.Logger, next http.Handler) http.Handler {
    38→	return http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
    39→		start := time.Now()
    40→		sw := &statusWriter{ResponseWriter: w, status: http.StatusOK}
    41→		next.ServeHTTP(sw, r)
    42→		log.InfoContext(r.Context(), "request",
    43→			"method", r.Method,
    44→			"path", r.URL.Path,
    45→			"status", sw.status,
    46→			"duration_ms", time.Since(start).Milliseconds(),
    47→			"request_id", RequestID(r.Context()),
    48→		)
    49→	})
    50→}
    51→
    52→// statusWriter wraps http.ResponseWriter to capture the status code.
    53→type statusWriter struct {
    54→	http.ResponseWriter
    55→	status      int
    56→	wroteHeader bool
    57→}
    58→
    59→func (w *statusWriter) WriteHeader(code int) {
    60→	if !w.wroteHeader {
    61→		w.status = code
    62→		w.wroteHeader = true
    63→	}
    64→	w.ResponseWriter.WriteHeader(code)
    65→}
    66→
    67→func (w *statusWriter) Write(b []byte) (int, error) {
    68→	if !w.wroteHeader {
    69→		w.wroteHeader = true
    70→	}
    71→	return w.ResponseWriter.Write(b)
    72→}
    73→
    74→// corsMiddleware adds permissive CORS headers for development. Production
    75→// deployments should restrict AllowedOrigins via configuration.
    76→func corsMiddleware(next http.Handler) http.Handler {
    77→	return http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
    78→		w.Header().Set("Access-Control-Allow-Origin", "*")
    79→		w.Header().Set("Access-Control-Allow-Methods", "GET, POST, PATCH, DELETE, OPTIONS")
    80→		w.Header().Set("Access-Control-Allow-Headers", "Authorization, Content-Type, X-Request-ID")
    81→		w.Header().Set("Access-Control-Expose-Headers", "X-Request-ID")
    82→
    83→		if r.Method == http.MethodOptions {
    84→			w.WriteHeader(http.StatusNoContent)
    85→			return
    86→		}
    87→		next.ServeHTTP(w, r)
    88→	})
    89→}
    90→
    91→func generateID() string {
    92→	b := make([]byte, 8)
    93→	_, _ = rand.Read(b)
    94→	return hex.EncodeToString(b)
    95→}
    96→

1→// Package vault defines the SPI for credential and secret storage.
     2→//
     3→// The vault stores sensitive values (API keys, OAuth tokens, service account
     4→// credentials) and injects them into connector execution requests without
     5→// ever exposing them to agents. Agents see only a credential reference ID;
     6→// the vault resolves the actual secret at execution time.
     7→//
     8→// The built-in implementation encrypts secrets at rest using AES-GCM with
     9→// a key stored in the environment. Alternative implementations can delegate
    10→// to HashiCorp Vault, AWS Secrets Manager, GCP Secret Manager, etc.
    11→package vault
    12→
    13→import "context"
    14→
    15→// Vault stores and retrieves secrets by path.
    16→type Vault interface {
    17→	// Get retrieves a secret by its vault path.
    18→	Get(ctx context.Context, path string) (Secret, error)
    19→
    20→	// Put stores a secret at the given path, creating or overwriting it.
    21→	Put(ctx context.Context, path string, value []byte, meta Metadata) error
    22→
    23→	// Delete permanently removes a secret.
    24→	Delete(ctx context.Context, path string) error
    25→}
    26→
    27→// Secret is a resolved secret value.
    28→type Secret struct {
    29→	Path     string
    30→	Value    []byte
    31→	Metadata Metadata
    32→}
    33→
    34→// Metadata carries non-secret attributes stored alongside a secret.
    35→type Metadata struct {
    36→	// Type classifies the credential (e.g. "api_key", "oauth_refresh_token").
    37→	Type        string
    38→	Environment string
    39→	// Labels are arbitrary key-value pairs for organization.
    40→	Labels map[string]string
    41→}
    42→

content.content:
1→// Package connector defines the SPI for execution connectors.
     2→//
     3→// A connector bridges the control plane to an external system — a payment
     4→// processor, a git provider, a calendar service, etc. When the policy engine
     5→// allows an action (or a human approves it), the control plane selects the
     6→// appropriate connector and calls Execute.
     7→//
     8→// Each connector implementation is responsible for one external system.
     9→// The SPI is intentionally narrow: connectors receive a bounded execution
    10→// request and return a result. Credential injection and parameter bounding
    11→// happen in the control plane before the connector is called.
    12→package connector
    13→
    14→import (
    15→	"context"
    16→	"sync"
    17→)
    18→
    19→// Connector executes an approved action against an external system.
    20→type Connector interface {
    21→	// Type returns the connector type identifier (e.g. "payments", "git",
    22→	// "calendar"). Used by the registry to route execution requests.
    23→	Type() string
    24→
    25→	// Provider returns the specific provider name (e.g. "stripe", "github",
    26→	// "google_calendar"). Multiple connectors may share a Type.
    27→	Provider() string
    28→
    29→	// Execute runs the action described by the request.
    30→	Execute(ctx context.Context, req ExecutionRequest) (ExecutionResult, error)
    31→}
    32→
    33→// ExecutionRequest is passed to a connector when executing an approved action.
    34→type ExecutionRequest struct {
    35→	GrantID     string
    36→	IntentID    string
    37→	ConnectorID string
    38→	// ActionType is the dot-namespaced action, e.g. "payment.charge".
    39→	ActionType string
    40→	// Parameters are the bounded, approved parameters for execution.
    41→	// These are constrained by the execution grant and may differ from
    42→	// the original intent if a human modified them during approval.
    43→	Parameters map[string]any
    44→	// Credential is the injected secret for this connector, resolved from
    45→	// the vault. Connectors must not store or log this value.
    46→	Credential *InjectedCredential
    47→}
    48→
    49→// InjectedCredential carries a resolved secret for use during execution.
    50→// Connectors receive this transiently and must not persist it.
    51→type InjectedCredential struct {
    52→	Type  string
    53→	Value []byte
    54→}
    55→
    56→// ExecutionResult is returned by a connector after execution.
    57→type ExecutionResult struct {
    58→	Status     ExecutionStatus
    59→	Output     map[string]any
    60→	ReceiptRef string
    61→	Error      string
    62→}
    63→
    64→// ExecutionStatus is the outcome of a connector execution.
    65→type ExecutionStatus string
    66→
    67→const (
    68→	ExecutionStatusSucceeded ExecutionStatus = "succeeded"
    69→	ExecutionStatusFailed    ExecutionStatus = "failed"
    70→	ExecutionStatusCancelled ExecutionStatus = "cancelled"
    71→)
    72→
    73→// Registry holds the registered connectors and routes execution requests.
    74→// It is safe for concurrent use.
    75→type Registry struct {
    76→	mu         sync.RWMutex
    77→	connectors map[string]Connector
    78→}
    79→
    80→// NewRegistry returns an empty connector registry.
    81→func NewRegistry() *Registry {
    82→	return &Registry{connectors: make(map[string]Connector)}
    83→}
    84→
    85→// Register adds a connector to the registry.
    86→// The key is "<type>/<provider>", e.g. "payments/stripe".
    87→func (r *Registry) Register(ctx context.Context, c Connector) {
    88→	_ = ctx // available for future database-backed implementations
    89→	key := c.Type() + "/" + c.Provider()
    90→	r.mu.Lock()
    91→	r.connectors[key] = c
    92→	r.mu.Unlock()
    93→}
    94→
    95→// Get returns the connector for the given type and provider.
    96→func (r *Registry) Get(ctx context.Context, connectorType, provider string) (Connector, bool) {
    97→	_ = ctx // available for future database-backed implementations
    98→	r.mu.RLock()
    99→	c, ok := r.connectors[connectorType+"/"+provider]
   100→	r.mu.RUnlock()
   101→	return c, ok
   102→}
   103→

1→// Package store defines persistence interfaces for the control plane.
     2→//
     3→// These interfaces decouple the API handlers from the storage backend.
     4→// The in-memory implementations (in the mem sub-package) are suitable for
     5→// development and testing. Production deployments swap in Postgres-backed
     6→// implementations without changing business logic.
     7→package store
     8→
     9→import (
    10→	"context"
    11→
    12→	api "github.com/ALRubinger/aileron/core/api/gen"
    13→)
    14→
    15→// IntentStore persists and retrieves action intents.
    16→type IntentStore interface {
    17→	Create(ctx context.Context, intent api.IntentEnvelope) error
    18→	Get(ctx context.Context, intentID string) (api.IntentEnvelope, error)
    19→	List(ctx context.Context, filter IntentFilter) ([]api.IntentEnvelope, error)
    20→	Update(ctx context.Context, intent api.IntentEnvelope) error
    21→}
    22→
    23→// IntentFilter scopes an intent list query.
    24→type IntentFilter struct {
    25→	WorkspaceID string
    26→	Status      *api.IntentStatus
    27→	AgentID     string
    28→	PageSize    int
    29→	PageToken   string
    30→}
    31→
    32→// ApprovalStore persists and retrieves approval requests.
    33→type ApprovalStore interface {
    34→	Create(ctx context.Context, approval api.Approval) error
    35→	Get(ctx context.Context, approvalID string) (api.Approval, error)
    36→	List(ctx context.Context, filter ApprovalFilter) ([]api.Approval, error)
    37→	Update(ctx context.Context, approval api.Approval) error
    38→}
    39→
    40→// ApprovalFilter scopes an approval list query.
    41→type ApprovalFilter struct {
    42→	WorkspaceID string
    43→	Status      *api.ApprovalStatus
    44→	IntentID    string
    45→	PageSize    int
    46→	PageToken   string
    47→}
    48→
    49→// PolicyStore persists and retrieves policy definitions.
    50→type PolicyStore interface {
    51→	Create(ctx context.Context, policy api.Policy) error
    52→	Get(ctx context.Context, policyID string) (api.Policy, error)
    53→	List(ctx context.Context, filter PolicyFilter) ([]api.Policy, error)
    54→	Update(ctx context.Context, policy api.Policy) error
    55→}
    56→
    57→// PolicyFilter scopes a policy list query.
    58→type PolicyFilter struct {
    59→	WorkspaceID string
    60→	Status      *api.PolicyStatus
    61→	PageSize    int
    62→	PageToken   string
    63→}
    64→
    65→// GrantStore persists and retrieves execution grants.
    66→type GrantStore interface {
    67→	Create(ctx context.Context, grant api.ExecutionGrant) error
    68→	Get(ctx context.Context, grantID string) (api.ExecutionGrant, error)
    69→	Update(ctx context.Context, grant api.ExecutionGrant) error
    70→}
    71→
    72→// ExecutionStore persists and retrieves execution records.
    73→type ExecutionStore interface {
    74→	Create(ctx context.Context, execution api.Execution) error
    75→	Get(ctx context.Context, executionID string) (api.Execution, error)
    76→	Update(ctx context.Context, execution api.Execution) error
    77→}
    78→
    79→// ConnectorStore persists and retrieves connector registrations.
    80→type ConnectorStore interface {
    81→	Create(ctx context.Context, connector api.Connector) error
    82→	Get(ctx context.Context, connectorID string) (api.Connector, error)
    83→	List(ctx context.Context, filter ConnectorFilter) ([]api.Connector, error)
    84→	Update(ctx context.Context, connector api.Connector) error
    85→}
    86→
    87→// ConnectorFilter scopes a connector list query.
    88→type ConnectorFilter struct {
    89→	WorkspaceID string
    90→	Type        *api.ConnectorType
    91→	PageSize    int
    92→	PageToken   string
    93→}
    94→
    95→// CredentialStore persists and retrieves credential references (not secrets).
    96→type CredentialStore interface {
    97→	Create(ctx context.Context, cred api.CredentialReference) error
    98→	Get(ctx context.Context, credentialID string) (api.CredentialReference, error)
    99→	List(ctx context.Context, filter CredentialFilter) ([]api.CredentialReference, error)
   100→}
   101→
   102→// CredentialFilter scopes a credential list query.
   103→type CredentialFilter struct {
   104→	WorkspaceID string
   105→	PageSize    int
   106→	PageToken   string
   107→}
   108→
   109→// FundingSourceStore persists and retrieves funding sources.
   110→type FundingSourceStore interface {
   111→	Create(ctx context.Context, fs api.FundingSource) error
   112→	Get(ctx context.Context, fundingSourceID string) (api.FundingSource, error)
   113→	List(ctx context.Context, filter FundingSourceFilter) ([]api.FundingSource, error)
   114→}
   115→
   116→// FundingSourceFilter scopes a funding source list query.
   117→type FundingSourceFilter struct {
   118→	WorkspaceID string
   119→	PageSize    int
   120→	PageToken   string
   121→}
   122→
   123→// MCPServerStore persists and retrieves MCP server configurations.
   124→type MCPServerStore interface {
   125→	Create(ctx context.Context, server api.MCPServerConfig) error
   126→	Get(ctx context.Context, serverID string) (api.MCPServerConfig, error)
   127→	List(ctx context.Context, filter MCPServerFilter) ([]api.MCPServerConfig, error)
   128→	Update(ctx context.Context, server api.MCPServerConfig) error
   129→	Delete(ctx context.Context, serverID string) error
   130→}
   131→
   132→// MCPServerFilter scopes an MCP server list query.
   133→type MCPServerFilter struct {
   134→	Status   *api.MCPServerConfigStatus
   135→	PageSize int
   136→}
   137→
   138→// ErrNotFound is returned when a requested entity does not exist.
   139→type ErrNotFound struct {
   140→	Entity string
   141→	ID     string
   142→}
   143→
   144→func (e *ErrNotFound) Error() string {
   145→	return e.Entity + " not found: " + e.ID
   146→}
   147→

1→// Package config loads and validates YAML configuration for the Aileron
     2→// MCP gateway.
     3→//
     4→// A configuration file defines the set of downstream MCP servers that
     5→// Aileron proxies, along with optional policy mappings and environment
     6→// variable overrides. Environment values prefixed with "vault://" are
     7→// resolved from the Aileron vault at launch time via [ResolveVaultEnv].
     8→package config
     9→
    10→import (
    11→	"fmt"
    12→	"os"
    13→	"regexp"
    14→
    15→	"gopkg.in/yaml.v3"
    16→)
    17→
    18→// namePattern matches valid downstream server names: starts with a letter,
    19→// followed by zero or more alphanumeric characters or underscores.
    20→var namePattern = regexp.MustCompile(`^[a-zA-Z][a-zA-Z0-9_]*$`)
    21→
    22→// vaultPrefix is the sentinel prefix for environment variable values that
    23→// should be resolved from the vault.
    24→const vaultPrefix = "vault://"
    25→
    26→// Execution mode constants for downstream servers.
    27→const (
    28→	// ModeLocal runs the MCP server as a local subprocess (stdio transport).
    29→	// This is the default when Mode is omitted.
    30→	ModeLocal = "local"
    31→
    32→	// ModeRemote connects to a centrally-managed MCP server over HTTP
    33→	// (Streamable HTTP transport). The control plane manages the server
    34→	// instance; no local command is needed.
    35→	ModeRemote = "remote"
    36→)
    37→
    38→// Config is the top-level Aileron gateway configuration.
    39→type Config struct {
    40→	// Version is the config file format version. Currently "1".
    41→	Version string `yaml:"version"`
    42→
    43→	// WorkspaceID is the default workspace for policy evaluation.
    44→	// Defaults to "default" if empty.
    45→	WorkspaceID string `yaml:"workspace_id"`
    46→
    47→	// DownstreamServers lists the MCP servers that Aileron proxies.
    48→	DownstreamServers []DownstreamServer `yaml:"downstream_servers"`
    49→}
    50→
    51→// DownstreamServer defines a single downstream MCP server.
    52→type DownstreamServer struct {
    53→	// Name is a unique identifier for this server, used as tool name prefix.
    54→	// Must be a valid identifier (alphanumeric + underscore, no spaces).
    55→	Name string `yaml:"name"`
    56→
    57→	// Mode is the execution mode: "local" (default) or "remote".
    58→	// Local servers are spawned as subprocesses; remote servers are accessed
    59→	// over HTTP via the control plane. Omitting Mode defaults to "local".
    60→	Mode string `yaml:"mode,omitempty"`
    61→
    62→	// Command is the command to execute, including any arguments.
    63→	// Required for local mode; must be empty for remote mode.
    64→	// Example: ["npx", "-y", "@modelcontextprotocol/server-github"]
    65→	Command []string `yaml:"command"`
    66→
    67→	// Env is environment variables to set for the subprocess.
    68→	// Values prefixed with "vault://" are resolved from the Aileron vault at launch time.
    69→	Env map[string]string `yaml:"env"`
    70→
    71→	// PolicyMapping configures how this server's tools map to policy evaluation.
    72→	PolicyMapping *PolicyMapping `yaml:"policy_mapping,omitempty"`
    73→}
    74→
    75→// PolicyMapping configures how tools from a downstream server map to the
    76→// policy engine's action type namespace.
    77→type PolicyMapping struct {
    78→	// ToolPrefix maps tool calls to the action type namespace.
    79→	// For example, with tool_prefix "git", a tool "create_pull_request"
    80→	// becomes action type "git.create_pull_request" for policy evaluation.
    81→	ToolPrefix string `yaml:"tool_prefix"`
    82→}
    83→
    84→// Load reads a YAML configuration file from path, unmarshals it, and
    85→// validates the result. It returns the parsed Config or the first error
    86→// encountered.
    87→func Load(path string) (*Config, error) {
    88→	data, err := os.ReadFile(path)
    89→	if err != nil {
    90→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("reading config file: %w", err)
    91→	}
    92→
    93→	var cfg Config
    94→	if err := yaml.Unmarshal(data, &cfg); err != nil {
    95→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("parsing config file: %w", err)
    96→	}
    97→
    98→	if err := cfg.Validate(); err != nil {
    99→		return nil, err
   100→	}
   101→
   102→	return &cfg, nil
   103→}
   104→
   105→// LoadFromEnvOrDefault loads configuration using the following precedence:
   106→//
   107→//  1. The path in the AILERON_CONFIG environment variable.
   108→//  2. "aileron.yaml" in the current working directory.
   109→//  3. An empty Config (no downstream servers) — this is not an error; it
   110→//     allows the gateway to start with no downstream servers configured.
   111→func LoadFromEnvOrDefault() (*Config, error) {
   112→	if path := os.Getenv("AILERON_CONFIG"); path != "" {
   113→		return Load(path)
   114→	}
   115→
   116→	const defaultFile = "aileron.yaml"
   117→	if _, err := os.Stat(defaultFile); err == nil {
   118→		return Load(defaultFile)
   119→	}
   120→
   121→	return &Config{}, nil
   122→}
   123→
   124→// Validate checks the configuration for structural correctness. It returns
   125→// a descriptive error for the first validation failure found.
   126→func (c *Config) Validate() error {
   127→	if c.Version != "" && c.Version != "1" {
   128→		return fmt.Errorf("unsupported config version %q: must be \"1\"", c.Version)
   129→	}
   130→
   131→	seen := make(map[string]bool, len(c.DownstreamServers))
   132→	for i, ds := range c.DownstreamServers {
   133→		if ds.Name == "" {
   134→			return fmt.Errorf("downstream_servers[%d]: name must not be empty", i)
   135→		}
   136→		if !namePattern.MatchString(ds.Name) {
   137→			return fmt.Errorf("downstream_servers[%d]: name %q must match %s", i, ds.Name, namePattern.String())
   138→		}
   139→		if seen[ds.Name] {
   140→			return fmt.Errorf("downstream_servers[%d]: duplicate name %q", i, ds.Name)
   141→		}
   142→		seen[ds.Name] = true
   143→
   144→		// Normalize and validate mode.
   145→		mode := ds.Mode
   146→		if mode == "" {
   147→			mode = ModeLocal
   148→		}
   149→		if mode != ModeLocal && mode != ModeRemote {
   150→			return fmt.Errorf("downstream_servers[%d] (%s): invalid mode %q: must be %q or %q", i, ds.Name, ds.Mode, ModeLocal, ModeRemote)
   151→		}
   152→
   153→		// Local servers require a command; remote servers must not have one.
   154→		if mode == ModeLocal && len(ds.Command) == 0 {
   155→			return fmt.Errorf("downstream_servers[%d] (%s): command must not be empty for local mode", i, ds.Name)
   156→		}
   157→		if mode == ModeRemote && len(ds.Command) > 0 {
   158→			return fmt.Errorf("downstream_servers[%d] (%s): command must be empty for remote mode", i, ds.Name)
   159→		}
   160→	}
   161→
   162→	return nil
   163→}
   164→
   165→// ResolveVaultEnv returns a copy of env with all "vault://"-prefixed values
   166→// resolved through the provided vaultGet function. Non-vault values are
   167→// copied unchanged. The original map is never modified.
   168→func ResolveVaultEnv(env map[string]string, vaultGet func(path string) ([]byte, error)) (map[string]string, error) {
   169→	resolved := make(map[string]string, len(env))
   170→
   171→	for k, v := range env {
   172→		if len(v) > len(vaultPrefix) && v[:len(vaultPrefix)] == vaultPrefix {
   173→			vaultPath := v[len(vaultPrefix):]
   174→			secret, err := vaultGet(vaultPath)
   175→			if err != nil {
   176→				return nil, fmt.Errorf("resolving vault env %q (path %q): %w", k, vaultPath, err)
   177→			}
   178→			resolved[k] = string(secret)
   179→		} else {
   180→			resolved[k] = v
   181→		}
   182→	}
   183→
   184→	return resolved, nil
   185→}
   186→

1→// Package app wires together the Aileron control plane components and exposes
     2→// them as an http.Handler. It is imported by the standalone server binary and
     3→// the MCP server's embedded mode.
     4→package app
     5→
     6→import (
     7→	"context"
     8→	"log/slog"
     9→	"net/http"
    10→	"os"
    11→	"time"
    12→
    13→	api "github.com/ALRubinger/aileron/core/api/gen"
    14→	"github.com/ALRubinger/aileron/core/approval"
    15→	"github.com/ALRubinger/aileron/core/connector"
    16→	googlecalendar "github.com/ALRubinger/aileron/core/connector/calendar/google"
    17→	"github.com/ALRubinger/aileron/core/connector/git/github"
    18→	"github.com/ALRubinger/aileron/core/connector/payments/stripe"
    19→	"github.com/ALRubinger/aileron/core/notify"
    20→	"github.com/ALRubinger/aileron/core/policy"
    21→	mcpreg "github.com/ALRubinger/aileron/core/registry"
    22→	"github.com/ALRubinger/aileron/core/store/mem"
    23→	"github.com/ALRubinger/aileron/core/vault"
    24→	"github.com/google/uuid"
    25→)
    26→
    27→// NewHandler creates a fully-wired Aileron control plane HTTP handler with
    28→// in-memory stores, seeded policies, and registered connectors.
    29→func NewHandler(log *slog.Logger) (http.Handler, error) {
    30→	ctx := context.Background()
    31→
    32→	// --- In-memory stores ---
    33→	intentStore := mem.NewIntentStore()
    34→	approvalStore := mem.NewApprovalStore()
    35→	policyStore := mem.NewPolicyStore()
    36→	grantStore := mem.NewGrantStore()
    37→	executionStore := mem.NewExecutionStore()
    38→	connectorStore := mem.NewConnectorStore()
    39→	mcpServerStore := mem.NewMCPServerStore()
    40→	credentialStore := mem.NewCredentialStore()
    41→	fundingSourceStore := mem.NewFundingSourceStore()
    42→	traceStore := mem.NewTraceStore()
    43→
    44→	// --- Connector registry ---
    45→	registry := connector.NewRegistry()
    46→	registry.Register(ctx, stripe.New())
    47→	registry.Register(ctx, googlecalendar.New())
    48→	registry.Register(ctx, github.New())
    49→
    50→	// --- Vault ---
    51→	v := vault.NewMemVault()
    52→	if token := os.Getenv("GITHUB_TOKEN"); token != "" {
    53→		v.Put(ctx, "connectors/github/default", []byte(token), vault.Metadata{
    54→			Type: "api_key",
    55→			Labels: map[string]string{
    56→				"connector": "github",
    57→			},
    58→		})
    59→		log.Info("seeded GitHub token into vault")
    60→	}
    61→
    62→	// --- Policy engine ---
    63→	policyEngine := policy.NewRuleEngine(policyStore)
    64→
    65→	if err := policy.SeedPolicies(ctx, policyStore); err != nil {
    66→		return nil, err
    67→	}
    68→	log.Info("seeded default policies")
    69→
    70→	// --- Approval orchestrator ---
    71→	idGen := func() string { return uuid.New().String() }
    72→	orchestrator := approval.NewInMemoryOrchestrator(approvalStore, idGen)
    73→
    74→	// --- MCP Registry client ---
    75→	registryClient := mcpreg.NewClient(nil, log)
    76→	if interval := os.Getenv("REGISTRY_REFRESH_INTERVAL"); interval != "" {
    77→		if d, err := time.ParseDuration(interval); err == nil {
    78→			registryClient = registryClient.WithRefreshInterval(d)
    79→			log.Info("registry refresh interval overridden", "interval", d)
    80→		} else {
    81→			log.Warn("invalid REGISTRY_REFRESH_INTERVAL, using default", "value", interval, "error", err)
    82→		}
    83→	}
    84→	registryClient.Start(ctx)
    85→
    86→	// --- Notifier ---
    87→	notifier := notify.NewLogNotifier(log)
    88→
    89→	// --- HTTP handler ---
    90→	mux := http.NewServeMux()
    91→
    92→	server := &apiServer{
    93→		log:            log,
    94→		registry:       registry,
    95→		policyEngine:   policyEngine,
    96→		orchestrator:   orchestrator,
    97→		vault:          v,
    98→		notifier:       notifier,
    99→		intents:        intentStore,
   100→		approvals:      approvalStore,
   101→		policies:       policyStore,
   102→		grants:         grantStore,
   103→		executions:     executionStore,
   104→		connectors:     connectorStore,
   105→		mcpServers:     mcpServerStore,
   106→		registryClient: registryClient,
   107→		credentials:    credentialStore,
   108→		fundingSources: fundingSourceStore,
   109→		traces:         traceStore,
   110→		newID:          idGen,
   111→	}
   112→	api.HandlerFromMux(server, mux)
   113→
   114→	registerDocsRoutes(mux)
   115→
   116→	// Middleware chain: CORS -> request ID -> logging -> routes.
   117→	var handler http.Handler = mux
   118→	handler = loggingMiddleware(log, handler)
   119→	handler = requestIDMiddleware(handler)
   120→	handler = corsMiddleware(handler)
   121→
   122→	return handler, nil
   123→}
   124→

content.content: Updated task #10 status

content.text:
All 10 tasks complete. Here's a summary of what was built:

## What's done

**Domain model** (`core/model/enterprise.go`):
- `Enterprise`, `User`, `Session`, `SSOConfig` types with prefixed IDs (`ent_`, `usr_`, `ses_`, `sso_`)

**Auth SPI** (`core/auth/spi.go`):
- `AuthProvider` interface — `Provider()`, `AuthorizationURL()`, `HandleCallback()`
- `Enforcer` interface — `IsProviderAllowed()`, `IsSSORequired()`, `IsEmailDomainAllowed()`
- `Registry` for resolving providers by name

**Store interfaces** (`core/store/store.go`):
- `EnterpriseStore`, `UserStore`, `SessionStore`, `SSOConfigStore` added alongside existing stores

**Atlas schema** (`core/schema/schema.hcl`):
- `enterprises`, `users`, `sessions`, `sso_configs` tables with proper FKs, indexes, and constraints

**PostgreSQL stores** (`core/store/postgres/`):
- Full CRUD implementations for all four new entities backed by pgx

**Google OAuth provider** (`core/auth/google/provider.go`):
- First `AuthProvider` implementation — Google OpenID Connect flow

**JWT + middleware** (`core/auth/jwt.go`, `core/auth/middleware.go`):
- HMAC-signed JWT issuance/validation, refresh token generation
- Bearer token middleware with skip paths for `/v1/health` and `/auth/*`

**Auth handlers** (`core/auth/handler.go`):
- `GET /auth/{provider}/login` — redirect to OAuth provider
- `GET /auth/{provider}/callback` — exchange code, auto-create Enterprise+User, issue tokens
- `POST /auth/refresh` — rotate refresh token, issue new access token
- `POST /auth/logout` — delete session, clear cookies

**Config** (`core/config/auth.go`):
- Env-based auth config (`AILERON_DATABASE_URL`, `AILERON_JWT_SIGNING_KEY`, `GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID`, etc.)
- Auth is **opt-in** — without `AILERON_DATABASE_URL` the app runs as before with no auth

**Wiring** (`core/app/app.go`):
- Conditionally wires PostgreSQL, auth registry, enforcer, middleware when DB is configured

**Tests** — 18 tests covering JWT, enforcer, and middleware — all passing alongside existing suite.

message.content: Will the schema changes auto deploy on Railway when the PR is created and merged?

content.text:
No — not yet. Right now the Atlas schema is just a declaration file (`core/schema/schema.hcl`). There's nothing wired to automatically apply it.

To get auto-deploy on Railway, we'd need one of these approaches:

**Option A: Atlas migration in the Dockerfile (simplest)**
- Add `atlas` CLI to the Docker image
- Run `atlas schema apply` as part of the container startup (or an init container/entrypoint script)
- The app connects to the same `AILERON_DATABASE_URL` that Atlas uses

**Option B: CI step in GitHub Actions**
- Add a workflow step that runs `atlas schema apply` against the Railway Postgres instance before/after deploy
- Keeps migration separate from the app binary

**Option C: Go-embedded migration at startup**
- Embed the schema in the Go binary and run Atlas programmatically (or use `golang-migrate`) during `NewHandler` / app startup
- No external tooling needed at deploy time

My recommendation is **Option A or C** — running the migration at app startup is the most operationally simple for a single-service deployment on Railway. Option C is particularly clean since it avoids adding the Atlas CLI to the Docker image.

Which approach do you prefer? Also — do you already have a PostgreSQL instance provisioned on Railway, or do we need to plan for that too?

message.content: Option A. Yes I have a PostgresSQL instance on Railway and the server service is conneted to it

file.content:
FROM golang:1.24-alpine AS builder

WORKDIR /src

# Copy module files first for layer caching.
COPY go.work go.work.sum* ./
COPY cmd/aileron-mcp/go.mod ./cmd/aileron-mcp/
COPY core/go.mod core/go.sum* ./core/
COPY sdk/go/go.mod sdk/go/go.sum* ./sdk/go/
COPY test/integration/go.mod ./test/integration/

RUN go work sync

COPY core/ ./core/
COPY sdk/go/ ./sdk/go/

RUN go build -o /aileron-server ./core/server

FROM alpine:3.20

RUN addgroup -S aileron && adduser -S aileron -G aileron
USER aileron

COPY --from=builder /aileron-server /usr/local/bin/aileron-server

EXPOSE 8080

ENTRYPOINT ["aileron-server"]

1→// Package main is the entry point for the Aileron control plane server.
     2→package main
     3→
     4→import (
     5→	"context"
     6→	"log/slog"
     7→	"net/http"
     8→	"os"
     9→	"os/signal"
    10→	"syscall"
    11→	"time"
    12→
    13→	"github.com/ALRubinger/aileron/core/app"
    14→)
    15→
    16→func main() {
    17→	log := slog.New(slog.NewJSONHandler(os.Stdout, nil))
    18→
    19→	if err := run(log); err != nil {
    20→		log.Error("server exited with error", "error", err)
    21→		os.Exit(1)
    22→	}
    23→}
    24→
    25→func run(log *slog.Logger) error {
    26→	addr := os.Getenv("AILERON_ADDR")
    27→	if addr == "" {
    28→		addr = ":8080"
    29→	}
    30→
    31→	handler, err := app.NewHandler(log)
    32→	if err != nil {
    33→		return err
    34→	}
    35→
    36→	srv := &http.Server{
    37→		Addr:         addr,
    38→		Handler:      handler,
    39→		ReadTimeout:  10 * time.Second,
    40→		WriteTimeout: 30 * time.Second,
    41→		IdleTimeout:  120 * time.Second,
    42→	}
    43→
    44→	// Graceful shutdown on SIGINT / SIGTERM.
    45→	ctx, stop := signal.NotifyContext(context.Background(), syscall.SIGINT, syscall.SIGTERM)
    46→	defer stop()
    47→
    48→	go func() {
    49→		log.Info("server listening", "addr", addr)
    50→		if err := srv.ListenAndServe(); err != nil && err != http.ErrServerClosed {
    51→			log.Error("listen error", "error", err)
    52→		}
    53→	}()
    54→
    55→	<-ctx.Done()
    56→	log.Info("shutting down")
    57→
    58→	shutdownCtx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 30*time.Second)
    59→	defer cancel()
    60→
    61→	return srv.Shutdown(shutdownCtx)
    62→}
    63→

file.content:
services:

  postgres:
    image: postgres:16-alpine
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      POSTGRES_USER: aileron
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: aileron
      POSTGRES_DB: aileron
    volumes:
      - postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    ports:
      - "5432:5432"
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U aileron"]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5

  server:
    build:
      context: ..
      dockerfile: core/server/Dockerfile
    restart: unless-stopped
    depends_on:
      postgres:
        condition: service_healthy
    environment:
      AILERON_ADDR: ":8080"
      DATABASE_URL: "postgres://aileron:aileron@postgres:5432/aileron?sslmode=disable"
    ports:
      - "8080:8080"

  ui:
    build:
      context: ../ui
      dockerfile: Dockerfile
    restart: unless-stopped
    depends_on:
      - server
    environment:
      AILERON_API_URL: "http://server:8080"
    ports:
      - "3000:3000"

  docs:
    build:
      context: ..
      dockerfile: docs/Dockerfile
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "3001:80"

volumes:
  postgres_data:

input.content:
#!/bin/sh
set -e

# Run Atlas schema migration if a database URL is configured.
if [ -n "$AILERON_DATABASE_URL" ]; then
  echo "Applying database schema..."
  atlas schema apply \
    --url "$AILERON_DATABASE_URL" \
    --to "file:///schema/schema.hcl" \
    --auto-approve
  echo "Schema applied successfully."
fi

exec aileron-server

FROM golang:1.24-alpine AS builder

WORKDIR /src

# Copy module files first for layer caching.
COPY go.work go.work.sum* ./
COPY cmd/aileron-mcp/go.mod ./cmd/aileron-mcp/
COPY core/go.mod core/go.sum* ./core/
COPY sdk/go/go.mod sdk/go/go.sum* ./sdk/go/
COPY test/integration/go.mod ./test/integration/

RUN go work sync

COPY core/ ./core/
COPY sdk/go/ ./sdk/go/

RUN go build -o /aileron-server ./core/server

FROM alpine:3.20

RUN addgroup -S aileron && adduser -S aileron -G aileron
USER aileron

COPY --from=builder /aileron-server /usr/local/bin/aileron-server

EXPOSE 8080

ENTRYPOINT ["aileron-server"]

#!/bin/sh
set -e

# Run Atlas schema migration if a database URL is configured.
if [ -n "$AILERON_DATABASE_URL" ]; then
  echo "Applying database schema..."
  atlas schema apply \
    --url "$AILERON_DATABASE_URL" \
    --to "file:///schema/schema.hcl" \
    --auto-approve
  echo "Schema applied successfully."
fi

exec aileron-server

content.text:
Here's what's set up now:

**Deploy flow on Railway:**
1. Railway builds the Docker image (which now includes Atlas CLI + schema file)
2. On container start, `entrypoint.sh` detects `DATABASE_URL` (auto-injected by Railway from the linked Postgres service)
3. Atlas runs `schema apply --auto-approve` — creates/migrates tables to match `schema.hcl`
4. The Go server starts

**What you'll need to set as Railway environment variables** on the server service:
- `AILERON_JWT_SIGNING_KEY` — any random 32+ character string (required to enable auth)
- `GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID` — from Google Cloud Console
- `GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET` — from Google Cloud Console
- `GOOGLE_REDIRECT_URL` — e.g. `https://your-server.railway.app/auth/google/callback`

`DATABASE_URL` is already provided by Railway automatically. Schema migrations will run on every deploy — Atlas is declarative and idempotent, so it only applies the diff.

content.text: Let me read the current README first.

content.content:
1→# Aileron
     2→_Stay on course. The missing protection layer between your agents and the real world._
     3→
     4→![GitHub License](https://img.shields.io/github/license/ALRubinger/aileron?style=for-the-badge)
     5→![GitHub Actions Workflow Status](https://img.shields.io/github/actions/workflow/status/ALRubinger/aileron/ci.yml?style=for-the-badge&logo=github)
     6→
     7→**Aileron is an MCP gateway that enforces governance over every tool call an AI agent makes.**
     8→
     9→It sits between an agent host and the MCP servers the agent uses. Aileron aggregates downstream MCP servers, re-exposes their tools, and intercepts every tool call for policy evaluation — without the agent ever touching credentials directly.
    10→
    11→---
    12→
    13→## The Problem
    14→
    15→AI agents are acting on our behalf: booking meetings, paying invoices, merging code. The problem isn't capability. It's trust. An agent that's useful is an agent that's risky.
    16→
    17→The current workarounds are inadequate:
    18→
    19→- Hardcoded rules buried inside agents, invisible to the people they affect
    20→- Manual approvals handled out-of-band in Slack or email, disconnected from execution
    21→- Over-permissioned API keys that agents hold directly
    22→- Static payment credentials exposed to systems that shouldn't see them
    23→- Agents can bypass governance tools entirely if they have direct access to APIs or other MCP servers
    24→
    25→The result is a forced choice: give the agent enough permission to be powerful, or restrict it enough to feel safe. Neither is satisfying.
    26→
    27→## The Solution
    28→
    29→Aileron is the **single MCP server** that an agent host connects to. It aggregates downstream MCP servers, re-exposes their tools under namespaced names, and intercepts every tool call for policy evaluation. The agent can't bypass Aileron because Aileron IS the tool surface.
    30→
    31→Agents remain autonomous in planning. We retain authority over execution. Aileron enforces the boundary between the two — by construction, not by cooperation.
    32→
    33→```
    34→Agent Host (Claude Code, OpenClaw, etc.)
    35→  │
    36→  │  MCP (stdio)
    37→  │
    38→  ▼
    39→Aileron MCP Gateway
    40→  ├── Policy Engine         evaluates rules per tool call
    41→  ├── Approval Orchestrator routes to humans when required
    42→  ├── Credential Vault      injects secrets at launch time
    43→  └── Audit Store           immutable record of every decision
    44→  │
    45→  ├──► Downstream MCP Server A (e.g. GitHub)
    46→  ├──► Downstream MCP Server B (e.g. Stripe)
    47→  └──► Downstream MCP Server C (e.g. Slack)
    48→```
    49→
    50→## How It Works
    51→
    52→**1. Agent host connects to Aileron as its only MCP server**
    53→
    54→Claude Code, OpenClaw, or any MCP-compatible agent host is configured with Aileron as the sole MCP server. The agent sees only the tools that Aileron exposes.
    55→
    56→**2. Aileron discovers tools from downstream MCP servers**
    57→
    58→On startup, Aileron connects to configured downstream MCP servers, discovers their tools, and re-exposes them under namespaced names (e.g. `github__create_pull_request`).
    59→
    60→**3. Every tool call passes through policy evaluation**
    61→
    62→When the agent calls a tool, Aileron intercepts it, maps it to the policy engine, and evaluates it against configured rules. The disposition is allow, deny, require approval, or allow with modifications.
    63→
    64→**4. Humans approve high-risk actions**
    65→
    66→If approval is required, Aileron holds the tool call and notifies approvers. The agent can poll with `aileron__check_approval`. When approved, Aileron auto-executes the queued call and returns the real result.
    67→
    68→**5. Credentials are injected from the vault**
    69→
    70→Downstream MCP servers receive credentials from the Aileron vault at launch time. Agents never see API keys, tokens, or secrets.
    71→
    72→**6. Everything is logged**
    73→
    74→Every tool call interception, policy decision, approval, and execution is recorded in an immutable audit trail. You have a verifiable record of what every agent did, who approved it, and when.
    75→
    76→## For Organizations
    77→
    78→Aileron gives organizations centralized control over agent activity across teams.
    79→
    80→- **Service catalog.** Configure which MCP servers are available to employees. Agents only see the tools you expose.
    81→- **Credential management.** API keys, tokens, and secrets live in the Aileron vault. Teams use agents without handling credentials directly.
    82→- **Policy governance.** Define rules that apply across all agent activity — spend limits, branch protections, vendor allowlists, time-of-day controls.
    83→- **Compliance.** An immutable audit trail records every tool call, policy decision, and approval for review and export.
    84→
    85→## Configuration
    86→
    87→Aileron is configured with an `aileron.yaml` file that declares downstream MCP servers, credential references, and policy mappings.
    88→
    89→```yaml
    90→version: "1"
    91→downstream_servers:
    92→  - name: "github"
    93→    command: ["npx", "-y", "@modelcontextprotocol/server-github"]
    94→    env:
    95→      GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN: "vault://connectors/github/default"
    96→    policy_mapping:
    97→      tool_prefix: "git"
    98→  - name: "filesystem"
    99→    command: ["npx", "-y", "@modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem", "/workspace"]
   100→```
   101→
   102→Each downstream server entry specifies the command to launch it, environment variables (with optional vault references for secrets), and policy mapping configuration.
   103→
   104→### Environment Variables
   105→
   106→| Variable | Default | Description |
   107→|----------|---------|-------------|
   108→| `AILERON_ADDR` | `:8080` | Address the control plane server listens on |
   109→| `AILERON_CONFIG` | `aileron.yaml` | Path to the configuration file |
   110→| `REGISTRY_REFRESH_INTERVAL` | `15m` | How often the MCP Registry server list is refreshed in the background. Accepts any Go duration string (e.g. `5m`, `1h`). The server prefetches the full registry on startup and refreshes on this interval. |
   111→| `GITHUB_TOKEN` | | GitHub personal access token, seeded into the vault at startup |
   112→
   113→## Current Status
   114→
   115→The MCP gateway architecture is implemented end-to-end:
   116→
   117→- **MCP gateway** aggregates downstream MCP servers and re-exposes their tools
   118→- **Policy engine** evaluates rules against every tool call (allow, deny, require approval, allow with modifications)
   119→- **Approval orchestrator** manages human-in-the-loop workflows with approve/deny/modify
   120→- **Credential vault** injects secrets into downstream servers at launch time
   121→- **Audit store** records every event in an immutable trace
   122→- **Approval UI** provides a web interface for reviewing and acting on pending approvals
   123→
   124→Five seed policies ship by default:
   125→1. Require approval for PRs targeting `main`, `master`, or `production`
   126→2. Allow PRs to feature branches
   127→3. Deny force pushes
   128→4. Require approval for tool calls with base argument targeting protected branches
   129→5. Deny destructive tool calls (`delete_*`)
   130→
   131→## Getting Started
   132→
   133→### Prerequisites
   134→
   135→- [Go](https://go.dev/dl/) 1.24 or later
   136→- [Node.js](https://nodejs.org/) 24 (see `.nvmrc`)
   137→- [pnpm](https://pnpm.io/) package manager
   138→- [go-task](https://taskfile.dev/) task runner
   139→- [Docker](https://docs.docker.com/get-docker/) and Docker Compose
   140→
   141→### Build
   142→
   143→```sh
   144→task build
   145→```
   146→
   147→This builds everything: Docker containers, Go server binary, MCP gateway binary, and the UI.
   148→
   149→To build individual components:
   150→
   151→```sh
   152→task build:docker   # Docker containers (server, UI, database)
   153→task build:server   # Go server binary
   154→task build:ui       # SvelteKit UI
   155→task build:mcp      # MCP gateway binary
   156→```
   157→
   158→### Run locally with Docker Compose
   159→
   160→```sh
   161→task up
   162→```
   163→
   164→To run in detached mode:
   165→
   166→```sh
   167→task up -- -d
   168→```
   169→
   170→This starts the control plane API server, the management UI, API documentation, and a PostgreSQL database. The API is available at `http://localhost:8080`, the UI at `http://localhost:3000`, and the API docs at `http://localhost:3001`.
   171→
   172→### Verify
   173→
   174→```sh
   175→task health
   176→```
   177→
   178→```json
   179→{"status":"ok","service":"aileron","version":"dev","timestamp":"2026-03-31T09:00:00Z"}
